title: else and elif
subtitle: Handle multiple outcomes with else and elif
learning_objectives: Use else to run code when an if condition is False
Use elif to test multiple conditions in sequence
Explain why the order of elif conditions matters

sections.heading: The else Clause
sections.content: An else clause runs its block whenever the if condition is False. You can think of it as the "otherwise" branch. else never has a condition of its own — it simply catches everything that the if did not.

sections.heading: elif: Testing Multiple Conditions
sections.content:
elif (short for "else if") lets you test additional conditions after the first if. Python checks each condition in order and runs the first block whose condition is True. Once a match is found, all remaining elif and else branches are skipped.

You can have as many elif clauses as you need. An optional else at the end handles any case not caught above.

sections.heading: Order Matters in elif Chains
sections.content:
Because Python evaluates conditions top-to-bottom and stops at the first match, the order of your elif branches is critical. If you put a broad condition before a narrow one, the narrow one may never be reached.

In the grade example above, placing score >= 60 first would incorrectly give every passing score a 'D', because 82 >= 60 is True and Python would stop there.
sections.code_example:
# WRONG order — any score >= 60 gets 'D'
score = 95
if score >= 60:
    grade = "D"   # matches first, even for 95!
elif score >= 90:
    grade = "A"   # never reached

print(grade)

exercises.title: Pass or Fail
exercises.prompt: Set score = 55. Use if and else to print "Pass" if the score is 60 or above, and "Fail" otherwise. Note: Both the if block and the else block need their print() lines indented by 4 spaces.
hints: The else clause has no condition — just else:

exercises.title: Day Type
exercises.prompt: Set day = 6 (where 1=Monday, 7=Sunday). Print "Weekend" if the day is 6 or 7, and "Weekday" otherwise. Use if/else.
hints: Saturday is 6 and Sunday is 7, so check if day >= 6
Alternatively you can use: if day == 6 or day == 7

exercises.title: Grade Calculator
exercises.prompt: Set score = 73. Use if, elif, and else to assign and print a letter grade: A (90+), B (80-89), C (70-79), D (60-69), F (below 60). Note: Each elif and else line starts at the same indentation level as if — not indented inside it.
exercises.starter_code:
score = 73

# Write your if/elif/else chain below
hints: Start with the highest grade first (A at 90+)
Make sure to print the grade variable at the end

tests.message: A score of 73 should produce grade C

quiz.question: What does elif stand for?
quiz.explanation: elif is shorthand for 'else if'. It lets you chain additional conditions after an initial if statement.

quiz.question: In an if/elif/else chain, how many branches will execute at most?
options: All branches that have a True condition
quiz.explanation: Python evaluates conditions from top to bottom and executes only the first matching branch, then skips the rest. At most one branch runs.

completion_message: Well done! You can now handle multiple outcomes using else and elif. Remember: order matters — put the most specific conditions first. Next, you'll learn about nesting conditionals inside each other.
